---
title: "Reconstruction Attacks"
subtitle: "Why answering too many queries too accurately reconstructs the data"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: white
    slide-number: true
---

## The setup

A curator holds a private binary vector and answers subset-sum queries. Each *accurate* answer
carves a slab out of the space of possible datasets. Enough slabs and only the true dataset
survives — that is the reconstruction attack.

> All figures and widgets below are produced by `libdpy`; this deck only embeds and frames them.

## Feasible region — 2 records {.smaller}

As queries accumulate, the feasible Boolean corners are eliminated one slab at a time.

::: {.content-visible when-format="revealjs"}
<iframe src="apps/reconstruction-2d-slab/index.html"
        width="100%" height="560" style="border:0"
        loading="lazy" title="Interactive 2-D feasible-region slab"></iframe>
:::

::: {.content-visible unless-format="revealjs"}
![Feasible region for 2 records: each query is a slab; their intersection shrinks toward the true dataset.](assets/slab-2d-static.png)
:::

## Feasible region — 3 records {.smaller}

In 3-D the surviving region is the intersection of slabs inside the unit cube.

::: {.content-visible when-format="revealjs"}
<iframe src="apps/reconstruction-3d-slabs/index.html"
        width="100%" height="560" style="border:0"
        loading="lazy" title="Interactive 3-D feasible-region slabs"></iframe>
:::

::: {.content-visible unless-format="revealjs"}
![Three-record feasible region: more queries shrink the surviving cube corners.](assets/slab-3d-static.png)
:::

## How many queries does it take?

{{< embed notebook.ipynb#fig-candidate-elimination >}}

## The query matrix

{{< embed notebook.ipynb#fig-query-matrix >}}

## Noise is the defense — at a cost

Adding noise to each answer thickens the slabs and blocks exact reconstruction — but degrades
utility. The tradeoff:

{{< embed notebook.ipynb#fig-reconstruction-error >}}

## Takeaways

- Exact answers to enough subset-sum queries reconstruct the data (Dinur–Nissim).
- The feasible region is an intersection of slabs; queries shrink it geometrically.
- Calibrated noise is what stands between utility and reconstruction — the heart of DP.
